statement: This target-owned planning record is not a RAPP/1 section 13 registry, trust anchor, authorization, signature, tombstone, genesis record, or external-mirror change. Candidate namespaces below remain unregistered until the estate owner completes and authenticates the applicable actions.

audit_evidence.scope_note: These reports describe the f71810 audit baseline and are evidence, not protocol authority or a claim about post-migration path state. Current facts recomputed after rebasing onto main are recorded separately in current_evidence.

current_evidence.status_state: Owner-action ledger links, target-owned launch containment, and the post-migration Active-path residual are present; the repository remains not fully conformant.

tail_check.input: the 16 RFC 9562 UUID octets of legacy_uuid

commons_invite.retired_reason: The value in the signature member is a non-JWS migration placeholder and the payload URL names the wrong Pages project.
commons_invite.address_note: The candidate address is exact only when the URL is the sole changed unsigned member. The signature member is excluded from the egg address, but any other unsigned-member change requires a new calculation.
commons_invite.observed_external_state: legacy brainstem-egg/2.3-neighborhood with the superseded 32-hex rappid; it is evidence, not the replacement

required_registry_surface.current_state: unsigned non-registry bytes; missing registry sequence, registry authentication, estate owner, append-only entries, SPKI, tombstone, re-anchor, and genesis records

semantic_differences: $.schemas_ref omits the canonical note that rapp-rappid/2.0 is former

kernel_latest_alias.classification: frozen v0.6.0 archive alias, not the active brainstem-v0.6.9 pin

facade_current.path_state: tracked regular file mode 100644; target-owned loopback-only post-migration pre-acceptance candidate corrected in this ledger's containing commit; not publicly approved

migration_state.inference_boundary_state: target-owned inference-refused default; adapter only through explicit dependency injection

facade_audit_baseline.current_interpretation: Historical audit baseline only. Current main contains the separate stateful target-owned facade, while these legacy routes remain immutable input or migration/containment evidence and do not become the public RAPP/1 wire.

candidate_namespaces.statement: These are review inputs only. Their presence in this ledger neither registers them nor permits authenticated acceptance.
candidate_namespaces.facade_error_code_registration_state: zero registered; every listed value is candidate-unregistered, including unknown-session

legacy_frame_kinds_requiring_owner_repla.current_migration_state: retained legacy planter is HTTP 410-contained; no current registration
retained legacy planter is HTTP 410-contained; no current registration
retained legacy planter is HTTP 410-contained; no current registration

required_registry_policies.spki: Resolve keyed rappids only through an authenticated spki entry, verify the DER-to-rappid tail binding, and deprecate the superseded entry at re-anchor.
required_registry_policies.genesis: Register every stream creation genesis; after re-genesis retain exactly one non-deprecated genesis for the stream, deprecate every predecessor, and preserve any old_stream_id to new_stream_id mapping.
required_registry_policies.tombstone: Append, never overwrite, an owner-authenticated revoked_utc decision and refuse affected artifacts at or after that instant while preserving earlier verification semantics.
required_registry_policies.succession: Determine the estate owner in effect at the artifact utc through append-only re-anchor records; verify historical artifacts against that tenure rather than only the current owner.
required_registry_policies.root_compromise: Recover an estate-owner root compromise only by redistributing a new anchor out of band, never by trusting the compromised registry to authorize its own replacement.
required_registry_policies.rollback_and_freshness: Persist the highest verified sequence, refuse lower sequences, refresh before tombstone or head-reset decisions, and report evidence older than policy as stale rather than clean.

actions.title: Publish the authenticated registry and out-of-band anchor
actions.issue_title: [Owner action] Publish the authenticated RAPP/1 registry and out-of-band anchor
actions.why: RAPP1_AUTHORITY.json is a structural pin only. No estate owner is designated, and the root rappid must not be assumed to be that owner. Without an independently selected and distributed keyed estate-owner anchor, matching SPKI, authenticated monotonic registry, and freshness policy, no signature, egg, frame, re-anchor, tombstone, or owner-tenure decision can be accepted.
actions.what: The owner must explicitly select the estate-owner rappid, without deriving it from the root identity, approve the candidate namespaces and valid replacements for legacy frame kinds, supply the matching public SPKI, authenticate an append-only rapp/1-registry at sequence zero or greater, distribute the anchor out of band, and authorize a separate publisher to advance the required kody-w/rapp-map surface with the signed monotonic bytes and freshness policy.

where.current_surface_state: unsigned rapp-ecosystem-spec/1.0 bytes, not a section 13 registry
where.constraint: Do not relabel specs/ecosystem-spec.json or RAPP1_AUTHORITY.json as the registry.

actions.when: Complete as one coordinated owner ceremony after namespace review, stream inventory, root re-anchor evidence, and external publication locations are approved; complete before any public authenticated acceptance.
how: Review every candidate protocol, egg-variant, error-code, kind, and family value in this ledger; enumerate any additional emitted kind explicitly because prefix inference and wildcards are forbidden.
Select the keyed estate-owner rappid independently and distribute it out of band; do not infer that rappid from the repository root identity or any structural authority pin.

Select valid label.label replacements and migration outcomes for memory_added, conversation, and tool_call in target-owned adapters or retirement records; never register those invalid legacy spellings as current kinds or edit installer/plant.sh.

Prepare in kody-w/RAPP a strict I-JSON unsigned candidate with schema rapp/1-registry, an owner-selected uint53 monotonic sequence of zero or greater, append-only entries, and exactly one current estate owner; label the export non-authoritative and do not overwrite either current ecosystem document.
Add SPKI binding, creation-genesis, re-genesis, tombstone, re-anchor, estate-owner, and master-plan state under the policies in this ledger.
Have the estate owner authenticate the canonical registry outside automation and publish the matching public SPKI; do not place any private key in this repository.
Distribute the self-certifying estate-owner rappid through an independently controlled out-of-band channel and record the approved channel in owner evidence.

Through a separately authorized external publication process, publish the authenticated bytes at kody-w/rapp-map main ecosystem-spec.json and record the immutable publication commit; target automation must not write that repository.
Persist the highest verified sequence in consumers and enforce the owner-selected freshness window.
prerequisites: Estate owner is available and controls an Ed25519 or P-256 master key outside the repository.
The root-upgrade evidence for owner-authorize-root-upgrade-reanchor is complete and ready for the same ceremony.
Every live stream and its sole intended current genesis is inventoried.
The candidate namespace lists have been reviewed against all deployed producers, including the facade candidate.
The three invalid legacy frame kinds have owner-approved valid replacements or explicit retirement outcomes.
The kody-w/rapp-map publisher separately authorizes the canonical-surface update.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Fetch kody-w/rapp-map ecosystem-spec.json through its immutable publication commit, parse with rapp1_core.strict_loads, require byte equality with rapp1_core.canonical_bytes, require schema rapp/1-registry and a uint53 sequence, then verify the detached unencoded JWS against the supplied SPKI after checking Hb(rapp/1:rappid, SPKI_DER) equals the out-of-band anchor tail.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Canonical parsing and cryptographic verification pass at the required surface, the protected kid is the anchored estate owner, and neither the prior ecosystem document nor a repository structural pin is used as a substitute.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Exact-match the authenticated entries against the approved protocol pin, all six egg variants, all six facade error codes, the memory/body/swarm families, and the three family-specific re-genesis kinds in this ledger.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Every approved value has one live entry, no omitted value is accepted, and any additional kind has an explicit reviewed family.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify the estate owner equals the independently distributed keyed anchor, not an inferred root identity. Verify memory_added, conversation, and tool_call are absent as registered current kinds and each has an owner-approved valid replacement or explicit retirement/migration decision.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Exactly one independently selected estate owner verifies, no root-owner assumption occurs, and none of the three invalid legacy kind spellings is accepted or registered.

acceptance_tests.procedure: For every keyed rappid and stream, verify the SPKI tail binding, exactly one live genesis, all deprecations, tombstones, root re-anchor, and owner-tenure intervals against the append-only entries.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No unresolved key, duplicate live genesis, missing deprecation, impossible tenure interval, or self-authorized root recovery remains.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Persist the accepted sequence, present a validly formed registry at sequence minus one, then present evidence older than the approved freshness policy.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The lower sequence is refused and the over-age evidence is reported STALE rather than VERIFIED or clean.

rollback_or_retirement.on_failure: Do not authorize external publication, increment, or claim authenticated acceptance. Keep the unsigned target export non-authoritative, all candidate namespaces unregistered, and RAPP1_STATUS blocked.
rollback_or_retirement.retirement_outcome: A published append is never edited or removed. Correct mistakes with a later authenticated append and explicit deprecation or tombstone; a compromised root requires a newly distributed out-of-band anchor.

actions.title: Authorize the historical root provisional-upgrade re-anchor
actions.issue_title: [Owner action] Authorize the historical root provisional-upgrade re-anchor
actions.why: The target changed the historical 32-hex species-root identity to the current 64-hex identity at unsigned commit 19ff7d9, but no owner-authenticated section 13 re-anchor record authorizes that migration. A self-asserted _migrated_from field and commit authorship are provenance, not authorization.
actions.what: The owner must explicitly select the lawful section 6.3 case. The 32-to-64 evidence supports upgrade only as a candidate; it is not inferred authorization. To close this action, the owner selects upgrade, supplies its case-specific evidence that the old provisional identity resolved to kody-w at read time, and includes the immutable authorization in the authenticated registry without replacing migration provenance. A different owner-selected case retires this candidate action and requires its own case-specific review.
actions.when: Select the lawful case and, if upgrade is chosen, approve during the trust-genesis owner ceremony before the current root identity is accepted as authenticated or used to authorize public artifacts. Do not assume the root identity is the estate owner.
how: Read rappid.json from the parent of commit 19ff7d9 and verify the exact historical stored ID, historical v2 provenance string, legacy UUID, repository owner, unsigned commit state, and commit lineage.
Canonicalize the historical ID on read to the lowercase provisional ID while preserving its 32-hex tail; do not emit that provisional ID.
Recompute Hb(rapp/1:rappid, UUID_octets) from 0b635450-c042-49fb-b4b1-bdb571044dec and require the exact current 64-hex tail already stored; do not mint another identity.

Require the owner to select the section 6.3 case explicitly. If upgrade is selected, assemble its evidence proving the old provisional ID resolved to kody-w at read time and bind that evidence to the exact old and current IDs; code and tail length must not select the case.

Have the applicable owner authenticate the re-anchor record outside automation. If this root identity is separately selected as estate owner, also require the additional outgoing-owner authorization before appending the record.
Append the verified record to the authenticated registry and preserve both existing _migrated_from strings and the migration commit.
Keep consumers fail-closed until the registry, anchor, SPKI, owner tenure, and this exact authorization all verify.
prerequisites: The out-of-band estate-owner anchor and SPKI ceremony is prepared.
The parent and result trees of migration commit 19ff7d9 are retained and independently reviewable.
The owner has reviewed all section 6.3 cases and will explicitly select the lawful case rather than allowing code to infer it.
The estate-owner selection is independently recorded; the root identity is not treated as estate owner by default.
No automation is delegated authority to approve, sign, or infer this action.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Require an explicit owner-provided selected_case. To close this candidate action require that value to be upgrade, then compare authorization old_rappid and new_rappid to the canonicalized provisional and current IDs and require the current tail to equal Hb(rapp/1:rappid, the 16 legacy UUID octets).
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The owner explicitly selected upgrade; every byte of both IDs, case, UUID, and derived tail matches; no code inference, alternate identity, or new mint appears.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Verify the applicable owner authentication under the estate-owner tenure in effect at authorization_utc and review evidence that the old provisional identity resolved to kody-w at read time. If the current root was separately selected as estate owner, verify the additional outgoing-owner authorization.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Owner authentication and upgrade-specific resolution evidence both pass, the conditional outgoing-owner requirement is satisfied when applicable, and commit metadata alone is not accepted.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare current rappid.json and git history to ensure both _migrated_from strings, legacy UUID, unsigned migration commit, and historical tree remain recoverable after registry publication.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No provenance string or historical object was rewritten, deleted, or presented as independent authorization; the false/unsigned commit state is not upgraded into protocol authentication.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Run the stdlib owner-ledger test and inspect the publication workflow for any path that derives approval from rappid.json, commit authorship, automation identity, or the unsigned ledger.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No automation path can create or approve the owner authorization, and this ledger remains candidate-only.

rollback_or_retirement.on_failure: Leave the current stored ID structurally visible but unauthenticated, block it from owner-authorized acceptance, retain the old IDs as migration evidence, and do not mint or silently restore another ID. If the owner selects a case other than upgrade, retire this candidate action and prepare a separate owner-reviewed case-specific action without automated authorization.
rollback_or_retirement.retirement_outcome: Once validly appended, the authorization is immutable. A later defect is handled by a new owner-authorized re-anchor or tombstone under section 13, never by erasing this migration.

actions.title: Reissue and retire the Commons invite
actions.issue_title: [Owner action] Reissue the signed RAPP/1 Commons invite and retire the placeholder
actions.why: pages/tutorials/commons.egg is a 443-byte RAPP/1 invite at address a03fa90289eaefcf1a6521cdc10ee17bc706a0bb353e688ad84135d684380fb7, but it carries a non-JWS migration placeholder and its Pages URL returns 404. The external .well-known invite is still a retired legacy schema with the superseded 32-hex identity. Neither can authenticate membership.
actions.what: The estate owner must authorize the Commons identity continuity, issue a new canonical rapp/1-egg invite for the current Commons rappid and live Pages URL, authenticate it under the owner tenure in effect at created_utc, record its new artifact path and address, publish it at the Commons well-known path and an approved target-owned path, then remove the placeholder artifact from all live use.
actions.when: Issue only after the authenticated Commons upgrade re-anchor and owner succession are available; retire the placeholder and switch tutorial links atomically only after both published replacement copies pass every acceptance test.
how: Build an exact seven-member rapp/1-egg manifest with variant invite, contents [], the current Commons rappid, owner-selected created_utc, and payload containing target_rappid, target_url, and target_kind only.

Set payload.target_rappid to rappid:@kody-w/rapp-commons:fea3bd6e80bbac79efc22c4c1185c276d1833925a037ce120330be35e2afc3c7, target_url to https://kody-w.github.io/rapp-commons/, and target_kind to neighborhood.

Require the owner to select the lawful Commons re-anchor case explicitly. Upgrade is only a candidate from the 32-to-64 evidence; if selected, authenticate the mapping from provisional tail 3929ce90ebe97fe2a95432e9f647f3a3 to the exact current Commons identity. The remote _migrated_from assertion and unsigned repack commit are provenance only.

Have the estate owner in effect at created_utc authenticate canonical(manifest without the signature member) outside automation; verify through the fresh registry succession rather than repository authorship.

Compute and record the section 9 egg address from the manifest without the signature member. If the URL is the only changed unsigned member, require d15305a25cbe6c9aab51a4ed2ab5514345772023a95d658b37fc19303e5778bc; signing alone cannot change the address.
Publish canonical JSON bytes at kody-w/rapp-commons:.well-known/neighborhood.egg and the approved target-owned path.
Update the tutorial to the approved path or canonical well-known URL, remove pages/tutorials/commons.egg from live distribution, and preserve its path and SHA-256 only in this ledger and git history.
Do not copy, normalize, or bless the currently observed external legacy invite as the replacement.
prerequisites: The authenticated registry contains both the explicitly owner-selected lawful Commons re-anchor and owner succession needed for created_utc.
The current Commons rappid and correct GitHub Pages project URL are owner-confirmed.
The kody-w/rapp-commons owner can publish the replacement at the well-known path.
The target-owned replacement path and publication commit are owner-approved.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Require canonical JSON, exactly the seven section 9.1 members, schema rapp/1-egg, variant invite, contents [], the current Commons rappid, and payload exactly {target_rappid,target_url,target_kind} with the values in this ledger.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The manifest and payload are exact, the URL includes /rapp-commons/, and no legacy schema or 32-hex identity remains.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Require an explicit owner-selected lawful Commons re-anchor case and verify its case-specific evidence. If upgrade is selected, require the exact mapping from provisional tail 3929ce90ebe97fe2a95432e9f647f3a3 to rappid:@kody-w/rapp-commons:fea3bd6e80bbac79efc22c4c1185c276d1833925a037ce120330be35e2afc3c7. Separately verify owner tenure for the invite.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The explicitly selected re-anchor case and owner succession both authenticate without code inference; the remote _migrated_from assertion and repository commit are not accepted as authorization.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Resolve the protected kid through the fresh authenticated registry, verify the detached unencoded JWS over canonical(manifest without the signature member), and require that kid to be the estate owner in effect at created_utc.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Both cryptographic verification and time-scoped owner tenure pass; a merely registered non-owner key is refused.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Recompute H(rapp/1:egg-manifest, manifest without the signature member), compare it to the owner-recorded egg address, and byte-compare the immutable external and target-owned publication URLs.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The address matches and both approved locations serve identical canonical bytes from pinned publication commits; if only the URL changed among unsigned members, the address is exactly d15305a25cbe6c9aab51a4ed2ab5514345772023a95d658b37fc19303e5778bc.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Search live tutorial, manifest, HTML, and download references for pages/tutorials/commons.egg and probe that old path after deployment.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No live reference remains, the old path is absent or explicitly retired, and no consumer can treat its former bytes as an accepted invite.

rollback_or_retirement.on_failure: Keep joining disabled and the old artifact retired; do not fall back to either the placeholder or the external legacy invite.
rollback_or_retirement.retirement_outcome: The placeholder bytes are removed from live distribution but remain identifiable by path, SHA-256, and git history. A future invite is a newly addressed artifact; it never mutates an already published content-addressed invite.

actions.title: Correct or retire the divergent rapp-god mirror
actions.issue_title: [External owner action] Correct or remove the divergent rapp-god ecosystem mirror
actions.why: The target claims byte-identical ecosystem mirrors, but the observed rapp-god object differs in bytes and still names rapp-rappid/2.0 where the canonical source names rapp/1. Moving main URLs provide no immutable provenance. Separately, rapp_kernel/latest is an unauthenticated v0.6.0 archive alias while the active immutable pin is brainstem-v0.6.9. Neither surface can support authority, current-release, or clean drift claims.
actions.what: The target must immediately mark or remove rapp-god as non-authoritative while the external owner decides whether to republish exact canonical bytes. The target must then pin immutable provenance for every retained ecosystem mirror and either retire the rapp_kernel/latest alias or add a target-owned provenance sidecar that identifies its unchanged bytes as the historical v0.6.0 archive, never the active pin.
actions.when: Resolve before the target uses any external mirror as provenance, authority evidence, or a public clean-status input; repeat verification whenever a pinned mirror is intentionally advanced.
how: Immediately remove or mark rapp-god non-authoritative in target-owned claims; external-owner correction is not a prerequisite for this fail-closed target action.

Open an issue in kody-w/rapp-god with audit file commit c6c0b3e2a68c96f8ed70005101f996ea91e4bd0e, later observed repository head 94d0f49800fdd94b627f089c9cf3d07a7774b89b, blob IDs, byte lengths, SHA-256 values, and two semantic differences recorded in this ledger.
If the external owner chooses correction, copy exact bytes from an owner-approved immutable kody-w/RAPP commit into api/v1/ecosystem-spec.json and publish a new immutable rapp-god commit.
If the external owner declines or cannot guarantee byte identity, remove rapp-god from active target mirror and authority claims and label the observed object divergent evidence only.

Regenerate target machine and human mirror declarations from their source; record repository, path, immutable commit, byte length, SHA-256, observation time, and verification result for every retained mirror.

For rapp_kernel/latest, either retire the mutable latest claim or publish a target-owned provenance sidecar pinning unchanged SHA-256 f7fb359bbe8b6ba3db3665d81cb8e573a266c716278d8d21d8962ea40821e5aa as the historical v0.6.0 archive; do not edit the alias payload or confuse it with brainstem-v0.6.9.
Make clear that ecosystem mirrors are not the authenticated section 13 registry and cannot replace the out-of-band anchor.
Do not write to any external repository from target automation or from this ledger implementation.
prerequisites: The target has authority to remove or mark its own rapp-god claim non-authoritative; external-owner agreement is required only for correction and reinstatement.
An immutable canonical source commit is approved.
The target generator for specs/ecosystem-spec.json and specs/ECOSYSTEM_SPEC.md is identified.
No external mirror is treated as trusted while the decision is pending.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Fetch each retained mirror through its immutable commit URL, compare byte length and SHA-256 to the owner-approved canonical source, and perform a byte-for-byte comparison.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Every retained mirror is byte-identical; otherwise rapp-god is absent from all active mirror and authority claims.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Parse the retained bytes and compare $.primitives[0].schema and $.schemas_ref to the canonical source.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The schema is rapp/1 and the former rapp-rappid/2.0 note is identical, or the divergent source has been retired from claims.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Inspect target machine and human declarations for repository, path, immutable commit, byte length, SHA-256, observation time, and status; reject moving-main-only evidence.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Every active mirror has complete immutable provenance and the two target renders agree.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Hash rapp_kernel/latest/brainstem.py, inspect rapp_kernel/manifest.json and public claims, compare them with KERNEL_PIN.json, and inspect the approved alias retirement or target-owned provenance sidecar.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The alias payload remains byte-identical at SHA-256 f7fb359bbe8b6ba3db3665d81cb8e573a266c716278d8d21d8962ea40821e5aa and is either not presented as latest/current or is explicitly pinned as the unauthenticated historical v0.6.0 archive; brainstem-v0.6.9 remains the distinct active immutable pin.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Inspect all updated claims for language or machine fields that would identify an ecosystem mirror as the section 13 registry or trust anchor.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No mirror is presented as authenticated registry state, owner authorization, or out-of-band trust.

rollback_or_retirement.on_failure: Quarantine the external mirror, remove it from active claims, retain the last verified canonical target pin, and retire the kernel latest/current claim unless its historical provenance verifies; never import divergent bytes or edit the kernel alias payload.
rollback_or_retirement.retirement_outcome: A removed mirror remains documented as historical drift with its observed commit and hashes. A retired kernel alias remains byte-identical in its versioned historical archive and is not presented as current. Reinstatement requires new exact-byte verification and immutable provenance.

actions.title: Approve the public RAPP/1 facade switch
actions.issue_title: [Owner action] Approve the public RAPP/1 facade switch after registry closure
actions.why: Current main contains the migrated stateful target-owned facade, but it remains pre-acceptance, its default inference boundary refuses closed, and its six candidate error codes are unregistered. The f71810 audit-baseline Tier 1 and Tier 2 handlers had no idempotency_key, Tether emitted an incompatible request, alternate capability routes could bypass the wire, and three claimed canonical identity doors returned 404. Publishing before the remaining gates close would turn a candidate into a false conformance claim and could route around the immutable-grail boundary.
actions.what: The owner must approve one public POST /chat route backed only by the target-owned stateful facade after registry and migration closure, supply a separately reviewed side-effect-free inference adapter through explicit dependency injection, prove all candidate error codes are registered, constrain error steps to the exact standard domain, route target-owned clients through the gateway, close or explicitly retire missing canonical-door claims, ensure alternate capability routes are unavailable, and verify that requests cannot import, mutate, mount, deploy, or execute through pinned grail paths.

where.current_source_state: tracked target-owned loopback-only post-migration pre-acceptance candidate

actions.when: Switch public routing only after all four RAPP1_STATUS owner blockers, the post-migration Active-path residual, canonical-door dispositions, exact current-facade tests, immutable pin gate, and owner deployment review pass on the same deployment commit.
how: Use the current migrated facade bytes identified in known_evidence.facade_current as the review baseline. Keep its target-owned inference-refused default until a separately reviewed side-effect-free inference adapter is supplied through explicit dependency injection; never import, call, mount, or edit the pinned brainstem Flask module.
Verify the fresh authenticated registry contains exact error-code entries for all six facade codes before routing any public request.
Require every emitted error step to be one of 1, 1a, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or null; registration of an error code does not permit any other step.

Inventory public routes and remove or make unreachable every direct-agent, alternate-chat, legacy business-function, browser-worker, and grail /chat route as a RAPP capability; retain only POST /chat plus control-plane GET /health.
Route target-owned Tier 2 and Tether clients through the facade and retire their direct incompatible wire use; do not change the pinned Tier 1 handler.

Verify the root canonical door serves the exact target rappid.json bytes and either publish each missing RAPP-owned identity door through separate authorization or retire its claim. Never modify or deploy kody-w/rapp-installer, its clone, or pinned files to close a door.

Deploy the facade on target-owned infrastructure with durable storage outside the grail paths and a rollback target that is unavailable or maintenance-only, never the legacy wire. Record the exact injected adapter bytes and prove the adapter cannot access grail token caches, telemetry, agents, tools, or facade persistence.
Run exact request, response, idempotency, concurrency, route-isolation, and refusal tests against the deployment origin.
Hash the three KERNEL_PIN.json frozen files before and after deployment and traffic tests, then run check_kernel_pin.py.
prerequisites: owner-publish-authenticated-registry is accepted and fresh.
owner-authorize-root-upgrade-reanchor is accepted.
owner-reissue-commons-invite is accepted and the placeholder is retired.
owners-correct-or-retire-external-mirror is accepted.
The cave, installer, and sample-session 404 canonical-door claims have approved publish-or-retire dispositions that respect the immutable installer boundary.
Target-owned Tier 2, Tether, and worker routes have approved gateway migration or retirement dispositions.
A side-effect-free inference adapter has been independently reviewed and is supplied only through explicit dependency injection; the default launcher remains inference-refused.
Current main migration commits through 4c2b999f8c890b76d057241d29ecda29e0239d79 are present, and every remaining Active-path residual has a reviewed closure or explicit fail-closed disposition.
The repository status claim remains blocked until every non-owner conformance gate also passes.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Extract PENDING_REGISTRY_ERROR_CODES from the deployment source and exact-match it to authenticated error-code entries and this ledger's six candidate values.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The three sets are equal with no missing, extra, aliased, or wildcard code.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Run python3 -m pytest rapp_brainstem/test_rapp1_facade.py -q, then repeat its success, malformed request, unknown session, unconditional terminal replay, in-progress, inference refusal, and storage refusal cases against the deployment origin.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Success has exactly response, agent_logs, session_id; every contract refusal is exact HTTP 422 with error.code and step null; every repeated same-scope idempotency key returns its original terminal bytes.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Extract every emitted error.step from source and deployment tests and exact-check each value against [1,1a,2,3,4,5,6,null], preserving the values as strings except null.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: No response emits an omitted, numeric, empty, aliased, or otherwise unrecognized step; the current facade's null value remains valid.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Enumerate the deployed router and probe /, /agents, /agents/invoke, /api/agent, /api/copilot/chat, /api/businessinsightbot_function, /api/trigger/copilot-studio, worker-invented endpoints, and the grail port from the public network; verify Tether and target-owned Tier 2 clients call only the facade.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Only POST /chat is a public RAPP capability; GET /health is control-plane only; every listed alternate route and the grail port are unreachable or return 404/410 without executing inference or an agent, and no target-owned client bypasses the facade.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Fetch the four canonical identity URLs in known_evidence.canonical_doors. Byte-compare the root response to rappid.json and inspect the approved publish-or-retire disposition for every 404 claim, including the installer immutability constraint.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: The root returns the exact SHA-256 59dd3b53e2ed0c7594b3754425938b907600fdf5787b1cef912276aa9d3711b3 bytes; every other claim is served from an authorized pinned publication or retired; no installer repository, clone, or pinned byte was changed or deployed.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Record SHA-256 for rapp_brainstem/brainstem.py, rapp_brainstem/agents/basic_agent.py, and rapp_brainstem/VERSION; prove the default launcher returns inference-refused without importing brainstem; inspect the injected adapter and exercise all facade cases; verify no grail token/cache read, telemetry write, agent/tool load, or grail path change, then rerun python3 check_kernel_pin.py.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: All three hashes remain the exact KERNEL_PIN.json values, the default inference boundary refuses closed, the launcher and adapter have no grail dependency or side effect, the pin gate passes, and facade state is written only to the approved external store.

acceptance_tests.procedure: Compare the deployment commit, authenticated registry sequence/freshness, closed owner actions, implementation gates, public documentation, health output, and RAPP1_STATUS.
acceptance_tests.pass_condition: Public routing and any authenticated/full-conformance claim switch atomically; otherwise health and documentation remain explicitly pre-acceptance and not fully conformant.

rollback_or_retirement.on_failure: Withdraw public routing to an unavailable or maintenance-only target, preserve durable idempotency state for investigation, restore explicit pre-acceptance claims, and never fall back to the grail or an alternate legacy route.
rollback_or_retirement.retirement_outcome: Registered error-code history is not reused or silently removed. A retired facade deployment is unreachable; replacement requires a new reviewed deployment commit and the same complete gate.
